explanation: The natural language statement "The self-test procedure shall confirm the switchover from external to internal power works" indicates a liveness property. It means that whenever the system is in the 'SelfTestMode', it must eventually achieve the condition where the test for power switchover passes. The 'G' (globally) operator ensures that this condition holds true at all times. The '->' (implication) links the 'SelfTestMode' as the antecedent to the consequence that the 'testPowerSwitchPass' must eventually happen, denoted by 'F' (eventually).

The natural language statement "The self-test procedure shall confirm the switchover from external to internal power works" implies a requirement that if the system is undergoing a self-test, it must eventually verify the functionality of the power switchover. The LTL formula captures this by stating that 'Globally (G), if the system is in 'SelfTestMode', then it must 'Eventually (F)' achieve 'testPowerSwitchPass'. This ensures that the successful confirmation of the power switchover test is a guaranteed outcome whenever the self-test procedure is active.

The natural language statement indicates that as part of the self-test procedure, the system must confirm the successful switchover from external to internal power. This means that whenever the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must eventually (F) achieve 'testPowerSwitchPass'. The 'G' (globally) operator ensures this property holds at all times.

The natural language statement "The self-test procedure shall confirm the switchover from external to internal power works" implies a requirement for the self-test mode. It means that whenever the system is in its SelfTestMode, it must eventually achieve a state where the power switchover test is confirmed to be successful. The atomic proposition "SelfTestMode" indicates the self-test procedure is active. The atomic proposition "testPowerSwitchPass" signifies that the test confirming the switchover from external to internal power has succeeded. The LTL formula `G (SelfTestMode -> F testPowerSwitchPass)` translates this by stating that "Globally (G), if the system is in SelfTestMode, then it must Eventually (F) lead to the testPowerSwitchPass being true." This ensures that the self-test procedure, whenever active, will always result in the confirmation of the power switchover's functionality.

The natural language statement specifies that during the self-test procedure, the system is required to confirm that the switchover from external to internal power works. This implies a mandatory outcome for a specific test within the self-test mode. The LTL formula captures this by stating that globally, if the system is in 'SelfTestMode' (the self-test procedure is active), then it must eventually reach a state where 'testPowerSwitchPass' is true (the confirmation of the power switch functionality is successful). This ensures that the test is not skipped and, if performed, it passes, thereby fulfilling the requirement to 'confirm' the functionality.

The natural language statement specifies that during the 'self-test procedure' (represented by SelfTestMode), it is a requirement that the 'switchover from external to internal power works' (represented by testPowerSwitchPass) is confirmed. This implies that if the system is in SelfTestMode, then eventually, the test for the power switchover must pass successfully. The 'G' (globally) operator ensures that this property holds true at all times. The '->' (implication) operator states that if the SelfTestMode is active, then the consequent must eventually occur. The 'F' (eventually) operator ensures that at some point in the future from the moment SelfTestMode is active, testPowerSwitchPass will be true, thus confirming the functionality as stated.

The natural language statement specifies a requirement during the 'self-test procedure' that the 'switchover from external to internal power works' must be confirmed. The atomic proposition 'SelfTestMode' directly represents the 'self-test procedure'. The atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass' indicates the successful confirmation of the switchover functionality. The LTL formula G (SelfTestMode -> F testPowerSwitchPass) captures this by stating that 'globally, if the system is in SelfTestMode, then eventually the test for power switchover success must occur'. This ensures that the confirmation is a necessary outcome whenever the self-test procedure is active.

The natural language statement "The self-test procedure shall confirm the switchover from external to internal power works" indicates a liveness requirement. It means that whenever the system is undergoing its self-test procedure (represented by 'SelfTestMode'), it must eventually achieve a state where the confirmation of the power switchover is successful (represented by 'testPowerSwitchPass'). The LTL formula captures this by stating that globally (G), if 'SelfTestMode' is active, then it is eventually (F) followed by 'testPowerSwitchPass'.

The natural language statement specifies a condition that must be confirmed during the self-test procedure: the switchover from external to internal power works. This is directly captured by the atomic proposition 'testPowerSwitchPass'. The LTL formula 'G (SelfTestMode -> F testPowerSwitchPass)' states that globally, whenever the system is in 'SelfTestMode', it must eventually be the case that 'testPowerSwitchPass' is true. This ensures that the self-test procedure eventually confirms the successful power switchover.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in 'SelfTestMode', then it must eventually (F) achieve 'testPowerSwitchPass'. This accurately captures the natural language statement 'The self-test procedure shall confirm the switchover from external to internal power works', by ensuring that if the self-test procedure is active, the confirmation of the power switchover (represented by 'testPowerSwitchPass') is an eventual outcome of that procedure.
